# UC-13.2.15 · Service Dependency Health Propagation

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that when one important service leans on another, trouble really shows up in the right order and strength across the chain, instead of hiding because a link was missing or the math waited too long.*

---

## Description

Validates Splunk ITSI service dependency directed acyclic graph health propagation so upstream producer outages depress consumer health scores with correct timing and magnitude. ITSI maintains an explicit dependency graph between business and technical services; health-score math walks that graph, weighting child KPI states into parents and fanning parent degradation across dependents when policies demand. This use case proves propagation is both mathematically consistent and operationally meaningful: consumers must actually look sick when a modeled producer is unhealthy, within expected latency budgets, without silent gaps from broken edges. It is intentionally narrower than UC-13.2.1 service health trending, UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode volume and mean time to resolve, UC-13.2.5 KPI base search performance, UC-13.2.6 correlation rules engine tuning, UC-13.2.7 predictive analytics, UC-13.2.8 glass table network operations views, UC-13.2.9 elastic pipeline throughput, UC-13.2.11 KPI threshold violation trending, UC-13.2.13 maintenance window governance, or UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold policies in isolation. The unique angle is graph integrity and propagation efficacy: orphan services, accidental cycles, asymmetric weights, stale parent-child links after template re-import, and aggregation policy mismatches that let parents stay green while children burn or the reverse.

## Value

Reliability leaders stop arguing about whether a red parent means anything when children still look green on executive dashboards, because the control produces evidence about dependency edges, lag, and weighting. Customer-facing teams receive fair prioritization when a producer outage genuinely starves downstream journeys instead of chasing ghosts from an empty `services_depends_on` field. Platform owners reduce audit findings after reorganizations by proving the service map, episode policies, and observability signals still line up across development, test, and production estates. FinOps and capacity partners see when dampened weights during blue-green cutovers intentionally slow propagation so change windows do not page the entire catalog. Finally, federated teams keep ownership accurate when services are disabled in one tenant yet still emit telemetry that confuses cross-region scorecards.

## Implementation

Export ITSI service and dependency inventory CSVs via REST using a read-only service account. Schedule uc_13_2_15_dependency_propagation_graph_audit every fifteen minutes over earliest=-4h@h. Multisearch gathers the dependency tree, itsi_summary KPI lanes, Performance tstats, and grouped-alert counts; joins add parent and child health, weights, and maintenance; a lag subsearch uses streamstats time_window=15m; eventstats adds percentiles; case() sets tiers. Page CRITICAL or HIGH rows to platform and service owners with Service Analyzer drill links.

## Evidence

Saved search uc_13_2_15_dependency_propagation_graph_audit with fifteen-minute schedule; weekly DAG audit dashboard; archived CSV exports of itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv; drill links to Service Analyzer and Episode Review.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab ITSI service tree, induce a parent-only KPI degradation with a known weight while children remain healthy snapshots; within fifteen minutes, itsi_summary shows parent health_score below sixty, the lag subsearch reports prop_lag_min at or above fifteen, severity_tier becomes CRITICAL_parent_unhealthy_child_healthy_15m when is_critical equals one, and episode_pressure rises if NEAP groups parent notables.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when parent and child health scores both exceed eighty-five, maintenance flags are clear, inventory weights match the template export, and itsi_grouped_alerts is quiet, the alert wrapper filtered to CRITICAL or HIGH should return zero rows for sustained intervals.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ITSI administrators who curate service templates, service analysts who validate dependency semantics, observability engineers who operate Splunk Application Performance Monitoring and OpenTelemetry pipelines, and episode managers who align Notable Event Aggregation Policies with the service graph. Licensing must cover ITSI on every search head that materializes KPI summaries into index=itsi_summary, plus Observability Cloud or on-prem APM when you cross-check RED signals against score propagation. Role-based access for REST exports uses a service account that can read /services/itoa_interface/service without interactive passwords; capability read_itsi_service or equivalent itoa read roles must be present before automation schedules fire. Network paths from search heads to KV store replicas, metrics indexes, and optional Observability Cloud ingress must stay within organizational latency budgets so snapshot exports complete before the fifteen-minute alert window closes.

Establish the authoritative service tree topology before tuning math: document which services are parents versus children, which edges are informational versus revenue critical, and which weights the business expects during steady state versus blue-green cutovers. KPI base search hygiene is mandatory because stale searches invent false parent-child skew; every KPI that feeds Service Health Score should have a documented owner, cron alignment with upstream data freshness, and skip monitoring in index=_internal sourcetype=itsi_searchlog when you expand this program later. Aggregation policy assignment must map service IDs to NEAP queues so episode propagation mirrors the DAG; orphan episodes that never attach to a service ID defeat the purpose of graph-aware paging. ITSI service template versioning belongs in Git or configuration management with hashes recorded next to each export so auditors know which template produced a given itsi_service_collection.csv row.

OpenTelemetry service.name mapping must reconcile to ITSI keys through a maintained translation table, not tribal knowledge, especially when Kubernetes workload names differ from business service titles. Optional Glass Table dependency views give executives a picture of the same edges this control tests; hyperlink those views from the weekly DAG audit dashboard. Lookup CSV ownership for itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv sits with the configuration management team; columns serviceid, owner, team, parent_serviceid, weight, is_critical, and maintenance_until must stay schema-stable or downstream joins break silently. Dependency DAG validation procedure should include periodic cycle checks, orphan detection against the REST inventory, and comparison of dev, test, and production exports on the same calendar day. REST endpoints /services/itoa_interface/service and related child resources backstop CSV freshness when lookups lag; episode policy alignment reviews should confirm severity ladders and suppression rules respect maintenance_until stamps coming from the inventory feed.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Materialize itsi_service_collection.csv from the itsi_service KV collection or a scheduled `| rest` export that captures service keys, parent keys, `services_depends_on`, enabled flags, and template identifiers. Parse dependency arrays with `mvexpand` or `spath` when JSON blobs arrive nested so each row expands into explicit child_sid and parent_sid pairs for multisearch arm tree. Refresh at least hourly in production and on demand after template pushes.

Lane par_lane queries index=itsi_summary with five-minute bins using stats latest(health_score) by svc_key _time to preserve the time axis for downstream lag logic and to mirror how operators eyeball Service Analyzer timelines. Lane chi_lane captures latest child KPI posture with severity snapshots for analysts who need to distinguish aggregate Service Health Score behavior from individual KPI severities. Lane perf_red issues `| tstats` against the Splunk CIM Performance data model keyed by Performance.host so you have a host-level RED proxy even when APM indexes live elsewhere; rename hosts to match entity translation rules. Lane nep_lane summarizes index=itsi_grouped_alerts counts per svc_key so episode pressure rides alongside graph results even though the primary pipeline filters to arm tree rows after multisearch completes.

Construct service-tree pairs by driving the tree arm from `inputlookup itsi_service_collection.csv`, coalescing serviceid, parent_serviceid, and any mirrored OpenTelemetry `service.name` aliases after joins. When `services_depends_on` arrives as a multi-value field, explode it, trim whitespace, and discard self-edges before stats. Compare health_score propagation timing by joining parent_sid to itsi_summary aggregates for parent_hs and child_sid for child_hs, then running the lag subsearch that applies streamstats time_window=15m on binned health series to estimate how long a child stayed healthy while the parent window showed an opening score above eighty-four and a closing score below fifty-eight.

Enrich each child with `| join type=left max=0 child_sid` against `inputlookup itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv` so owner, team, weight, is_critical, and maintenance_until flow into case logic. Throttle join cardinality by ensuring inventory serviceid keys are unique; dedupe upstream if mergers created duplicates. Maintain optional Observability Cloud and APM exports in separate indexes if this search later expands; for now Performance.tstats supplies infrastructure-level corroboration.

props.conf guidance stays light but timestamp alignment matters: itsi_summary events must carry accurate _time versus indexed time or lag math invents false positives. When HTTP Event Collector feeds Observability data, confirm time zones match ITSI search heads.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_15_dependency_propagation_graph_audit with a fifteen-minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert actions gated on severity_tier matching CRITICAL_parent_unhealthy_child_healthy_15m or HIGH_propagation_lag_5_15m. Throttle duplicate child_sid, parent_sid pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from HIGH to CRITICAL. Include recommended_next tokens in pager descriptions so responders open Service Analyzer, Episode Review, KPI base search editors, or the dependency inventory pull request as appropriate.

#### Understanding this SPL: the backtick comment macro documents tunables. multisearch fans five arms so one silent lane cannot hide outages: tree inventory, binned parent lane summaries, child snapshot lane, Performance RED proxy lane, and grouped alert pressure lane. The outer where clause keeps only tree rows while prior arms still executed for coverage logging in Job Inspector. coalesce() unifies service identifiers across serviceid, service_id, itsi_service_id, parent keys, and unified_svc labels. join wraps `inputlookup itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv` with the mandated columns. streamstats appears both in the lag subsearch with time_window=15m and in the outer window=4 pass to sequence parent and child health for multi-edge children. eventstats derives org_p90_prop per team plus org_p50_prop_all for percentile context. case() encodes CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, and INFO tiers with maintenance precedence. tstats hits the Performance data model twice: once inside multisearch for perf_red and once after joins for child-level cpu_avg enrichment, which also satisfies redundancy if an arm is disabled in clones.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.15 ITSI dependency health propagation graph: tree inventory, parent-child health lag, Performance RED proxy, NEAP fan-out; tunables earliest=-4h@h parent_sick_lt=60 child_ok_gt=85 crit_lag_min=15")`
| multisearch
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_collection.csv
      | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval parent_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_serviceid,parent_service_id,parent_key,"")))
      | eval arm="tree"
      | eval _time=now()
      | where len(child_sid)>0 ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | where len(svc_key)>0
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs_pr BY svc_key _time
      | eval arm="par_lane" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs_cs latest(severity) AS kpi_sev_now BY svc_key
      | eval arm="chi_lane"
      | eval _time=now() ]
    [| tstats summariesonly=f avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_avg count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS svc_key
      | eval arm="perf_red"
      | eval _time=now() ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,itsi_service_id,serviceid,"")))
      | stats count AS neap_cnt BY svc_key
      | eval arm="nep_lane"
      | eval _time=now() ]
| where arm="tree"
| join type=left max=0 parent_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS parent_hs latest(_time) AS parent_seen BY svc_key
      | rename svc_key AS parent_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS child_hs latest(_time) AS child_seen BY svc_key
      | rename svc_key AS child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv
     | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
     | eval owner=trim(toString(coalesce(owner,"unassigned")))
     | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(team,"unassigned")))
     | eval parent_serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_serviceid,parent_key,"")))
     | eval weight=tonumber(coalesce(weight,"1"))
     | eval is_critical=tonumber(coalesce(is_critical,"0"))
     | eval maintenance_until=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_until,"")))
     | fields child_sid owner team parent_serviceid weight is_critical maintenance_until ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [| tstats summariesonly=f avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_avg count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,itsi_service_id,serviceid,"")))
      | stats count AS episode_pressure BY child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs BY svc_key _time
      | sort 0 svc_key + _time
      | streamstats time_window=15m global=f window=600 current=t last(hs) AS hs_now first(hs) AS hs_open BY svc_key
      | where hs_open>84 AND hs_now<58
      | stats latest(_time) AS breach_ts BY svc_key
      | eval prop_lag_min=round((now()-breach_ts)/60,2)
      | rename svc_key AS child_sid
      | fields child_sid prop_lag_min ]
| eval parent_svc_id=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_sid,parent_serviceid,"")))
| eval unified_svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(child_sid,parent_svc_id,""))))
| eval parent_hs=tonumber(coalesce(parent_hs,100))
| eval child_hs=tonumber(coalesce(child_hs,100))
| eval is_critical=tonumber(coalesce(is_critical,0))
| eval weight=tonumber(coalesce(weight,1))
| eval maint_flag=if(len(maintenance_until)>8,1,0)
| eval parent_unhealthy=if(parent_hs<60,1,0)
| eval child_healthy=if(child_hs>85,1,0)
| eval prop_lag_min=tonumber(coalesce(prop_lag_min,0))
| sort 0 child_sid parent_sid
| streamstats window=4 global=f current=t first(parent_hs) AS ph_run last(child_hs) AS ch_run BY child_sid
| eventstats perc90(prop_lag_min) AS org_p90_prop BY team
| eventstats perc50(prop_lag_min) AS org_p50_prop_all
| eval severity_tier=case(
    maint_flag==1,"INFO_maintenance_window_active",
    parent_unhealthy==1 AND child_healthy==1 AND is_critical==1 AND prop_lag_min>=15,"CRITICAL_parent_unhealthy_child_healthy_15m",
    parent_unhealthy==1 AND prop_lag_min>=5 AND prop_lag_min<15,"HIGH_propagation_lag_5_15m",
    weight>=1.85 AND parent_unhealthy==1,"MEDIUM_weight_asymmetry",
    len(parent_sid)<2 OR match(parent_sid,"^unknown$"),"LOW_orphan_service",
    true(),"INFO_watch")
| eval red_hint=if(tonumber(coalesce(cpu_avg,0))>93 OR tonumber(coalesce(perf_hits,0))==0,1,0)
| eval parent_child_align=if(parent_svc_id==parent_sid OR parent_serviceid==parent_sid,1,0)
| eval last_seen=coalesce(child_seen,parent_seen,now())
| eval recommended_next=case(
    match(severity_tier,"CRITICAL"),"invoke_service_analyzer_drill_and_open_episode_review_for_dependency_chain",
    match(severity_tier,"HIGH"),"compare_kpi_base_searches_between_parent_and_child_then_check_neap_assignment",
    match(severity_tier,"MEDIUM"),"reconcile_dependency_weight_table_and_rerun_health_score_math_preview",
    match(severity_tier,"LOW"),"repair_parent_child_link_or_refresh_service_template_export",
    true(),"record_result_in_weekly_dag_audit_dashboard")
| table child_sid parent_sid parent_serviceid parent_hs child_hs owner team weight is_critical maintenance_until episode_pressure prop_lag_min org_p90_prop org_p50_prop_all red_hint severity_tier recommended_next last_seen unified_svc parent_child_align
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[uc_13_2_15_dependency_propagation_graph_audit_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_15_dependency_propagation_graph_audit | where like(severity_tier,"CRITICAL%") OR like(severity_tier,"HIGH%")

### Step 4 — Validate

Induce a parent-only outage in a non-production ITSI service tree by lowering a parent KPI threshold or pausing a benign parent base search under change control, then measure propagation latency until child health_score reflects impact or the lag subsearch crosses five minutes. Compare Splunk rows to Service Analyzer curves for the same service IDs and capture screenshots for the change record.

Validate severity classification by forcing is_critical equals one on a test edge with weights above neutral so CRITICAL_parent_unhealthy_child_healthy_15m fires only after fifteen minutes of modeled lag, then clear the fault and confirm severity returns to INFO_watch within two windows. Cross-check episode_pressure against Episode Review for the same parent notables to prove NEAP assignments align with the graph.

Operationalize alert routing to ITSI administrators and owning teams using roster metadata from the inventory lookup; escalate to Head of Platform when org_p90_prop exceeds organizational service-level objectives for more than three consecutive runs. Publish a weekly DAG audit dashboard that lists orphan services, weight outliers, parent-child alignment mismatches, and Observability RED divergences. Integrate the dashboard iframe or deep link beside the Glass Table network operations view so executives see the same edges operators test. Automate REST-driven ticket creation for orphan services when LOW_orphan_service appears, including the parent_serviceid column and last exporter timestamp. Wire maintenance window automatic suppression by honoring maintenance_until from the inventory feed and ITSI maintenance flags together so benign INFO_maintenance_window_active rows do not spam executives.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — `services_depends_on` field empty after template re-import: restore the latest Git-exported template, rerun the KV export job, and verify REST shows dependencies before reopening episodes tied to the service.

Case B — Duplicate parent_serviceid via service rename: dedupe inventory rows, reconcile GUID history in the KV store, and update itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv so joins stop duplicating weight multipliers.

Case C — Orphan child service after team reorg: map the child to its new parent in ITSI, refresh exports, and close stale Glass Table links that still visualize the old edge.

Case D — Weight asymmetry causing health math drift: replay health score calculations with documented weights, compare to Splunk rows tagged MEDIUM_weight_asymmetry, and adjust business weightings under CAB approval.

Case E — Episode aggregation policy not assigned: bind the service ID to the correct NEAP, replay notables, and confirm itsi_grouped_alerts counts move from zero to proportional values.

Case F — Maintenance window not honored: align ITSI maintenance objects with maintenance_until strings from inventory, fix time zones, and pause external Observability alerts that lack the same suppression.

Case G — ITSI summary index acceleration paused: restore acceleration on itsi_summary summaries, verify tstats paths, and delay paging until search performance recovers.

Case H — KPI base search schedule misaligned with parent: reschedule child searches to finish before parent rollups, reduce search load, or shard heavy KPIs so parent_hs and child_hs timestamps stay coherent.

Case I — Dual-stack ITSI 4.x versus 4.18 inventory mismatch: run both REST export formats in parallel, compare DAG cycle reports, and standardize on the newer validation API for production audits.

Case J — Cyclic dependency introduced via service template: break the cycle in the template, redeploy, and document why the cycle broke score propagation math for future reviewers.

Case K — REST API token without read_itsi_service capability: rotate to a compliant service account, replay exports, and annotate the runbook so automation stops failing silently on HTTP forbidden responses.

Performance baselining belongs in the same program: capture median cpu_avg from the Performance join during golden weeks so red_hint triggers compare to an empirical host baseline rather than a single static ninety-three percent threshold when your estate normally runs hotter. Document Splunk Observability Cloud Add-on index macros if perf_red arms are retargeted at cloud metrics indexes, and keep Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry field extractions aligned to `service.name` so unified_svc stays stable across hybrid estates. When Splunk Service Intelligence Content Pack objects introduce auxiliary services, reconcile their template IDs against this audit so content-pack imports never overwrite production weights without a ticket.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.15 ITSI dependency health propagation graph: tree inventory, parent-child health lag, Performance RED proxy, NEAP fan-out; tunables earliest=-4h@h parent_sick_lt=60 child_ok_gt=85 crit_lag_min=15")`
| multisearch
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_collection.csv
      | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval parent_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_serviceid,parent_service_id,parent_key,"")))
      | eval arm="tree"
      | eval _time=now()
      | where len(child_sid)>0 ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | where len(svc_key)>0
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs_pr BY svc_key _time
      | eval arm="par_lane" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs_cs latest(severity) AS kpi_sev_now BY svc_key
      | eval arm="chi_lane"
      | eval _time=now() ]
    [| tstats summariesonly=f avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_avg count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS svc_key
      | eval arm="perf_red"
      | eval _time=now() ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,itsi_service_id,serviceid,"")))
      | stats count AS neap_cnt BY svc_key
      | eval arm="nep_lane"
      | eval _time=now() ]
| where arm="tree"
| join type=left max=0 parent_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS parent_hs latest(_time) AS parent_seen BY svc_key
      | rename svc_key AS parent_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | stats latest(health_score) AS child_hs latest(_time) AS child_seen BY svc_key
      | rename svc_key AS child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_dependency_inventory.csv
     | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
     | eval owner=trim(toString(coalesce(owner,"unassigned")))
     | eval team=trim(toString(coalesce(team,"unassigned")))
     | eval parent_serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_serviceid,parent_key,"")))
     | eval weight=tonumber(coalesce(weight,"1"))
     | eval is_critical=tonumber(coalesce(is_critical,"0"))
     | eval maintenance_until=trim(toString(coalesce(maintenance_until,"")))
     | fields child_sid owner team parent_serviceid weight is_critical maintenance_until ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [| tstats summariesonly=f avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_avg count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval child_sid=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,itsi_service_id,serviceid,"")))
      | stats count AS episode_pressure BY child_sid ]
| join type=left max=0 child_sid
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc_key=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | bin _time span=5m
      | stats latest(health_score) AS hs BY svc_key _time
      | sort 0 svc_key + _time
      | streamstats time_window=15m global=f window=600 current=t last(hs) AS hs_now first(hs) AS hs_open BY svc_key
      | where hs_open>84 AND hs_now<58
      | stats latest(_time) AS breach_ts BY svc_key
      | eval prop_lag_min=round((now()-breach_ts)/60,2)
      | rename svc_key AS child_sid
      | fields child_sid prop_lag_min ]
| eval parent_svc_id=trim(toString(coalesce(parent_sid,parent_serviceid,"")))
| eval unified_svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(child_sid,parent_svc_id,""))))
| eval parent_hs=tonumber(coalesce(parent_hs,100))
| eval child_hs=tonumber(coalesce(child_hs,100))
| eval is_critical=tonumber(coalesce(is_critical,0))
| eval weight=tonumber(coalesce(weight,1))
| eval maint_flag=if(len(maintenance_until)>8,1,0)
| eval parent_unhealthy=if(parent_hs<60,1,0)
| eval child_healthy=if(child_hs>85,1,0)
| eval prop_lag_min=tonumber(coalesce(prop_lag_min,0))
| sort 0 child_sid parent_sid
| streamstats window=4 global=f current=t first(parent_hs) AS ph_run last(child_hs) AS ch_run BY child_sid
| eventstats perc90(prop_lag_min) AS org_p90_prop BY team
| eventstats perc50(prop_lag_min) AS org_p50_prop_all
| eval severity_tier=case(
    maint_flag==1,"INFO_maintenance_window_active",
    parent_unhealthy==1 AND child_healthy==1 AND is_critical==1 AND prop_lag_min>=15,"CRITICAL_parent_unhealthy_child_healthy_15m",
    parent_unhealthy==1 AND prop_lag_min>=5 AND prop_lag_min<15,"HIGH_propagation_lag_5_15m",
    weight>=1.85 AND parent_unhealthy==1,"MEDIUM_weight_asymmetry",
    len(parent_sid)<2 OR match(parent_sid,"^unknown$"),"LOW_orphan_service",
    true(),"INFO_watch")
| eval red_hint=if(tonumber(coalesce(cpu_avg,0))>93 OR tonumber(coalesce(perf_hits,0))==0,1,0)
| eval parent_child_align=if(parent_svc_id==parent_sid OR parent_serviceid==parent_sid,1,0)
| eval last_seen=coalesce(child_seen,parent_seen,now())
| eval recommended_next=case(
    match(severity_tier,"CRITICAL"),"invoke_service_analyzer_drill_and_open_episode_review_for_dependency_chain",
    match(severity_tier,"HIGH"),"compare_kpi_base_searches_between_parent_and_child_then_check_neap_assignment",
    match(severity_tier,"MEDIUM"),"reconcile_dependency_weight_table_and_rerun_health_score_math_preview",
    match(severity_tier,"LOW"),"repair_parent_child_link_or_refresh_service_template_export",
    true(),"record_result_in_weekly_dag_audit_dashboard")
| table child_sid parent_sid parent_serviceid parent_hs child_hs owner team weight is_critical maintenance_until episode_pressure prop_lag_min org_p90_prop org_p50_prop_all red_hint severity_tier recommended_next last_seen unified_svc parent_child_align
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS svc_host
| table svc_host cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Directed graph or Sankey of parent-child edges with color by severity_tier, side table of prop_lag_min versus org_p90_prop, episode_pressure sparkline from itsi_grouped_alerts, and drilldowns to Service Analyzer, Episode Review, APM service map, and Observability Cloud service map panels.

## Known False Positives

Template re-import after a service rename often clears `services_depends_on` until the nightly DAG export runs, which looks like a propagation failure even when runtime health is fine. Drift between development, test, and production ITSI deployments produces mismatched parent keys while Observability Cloud still shows a single global service name, so RED telemetry disagrees with score math until inventories reconcile. Parent health-score recalculation legitimately lags during bulk service updates or backup-restore windows, creating minutes where parents look healthy while child KPIs already reflect outages. Deliberate dampening weights during blue-green cutovers suppress propagation on purpose; treat those windows as governed change, not defects. KPI base searches on a stale cron cadence can leave child snapshots fresher than parent snapshots, inventing false lag until schedules align. Maintenance windows in ITSI suppress severity while Observability Cloud keeps firing, so the joint search may show INFO_maintenance_window_active beside noisy external alerts. Sub-service entity merges collapse duplicate nodes and temporarily orphan historical edges until analysts remap dependencies. ITSI 4.x inventories exported through legacy REST shapes disagree with 4.18 and newer DAG validation helpers, so automated cycle detection may flag benign differences across versions. Federated ownership sometimes marks `enabled=0` in one tenant while Universal Forwarders still emit entity telemetry tied to retired keys, which mimics orphan services until ingest filters catch up.
